page: Microsoft Azure D8as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing for external block storage attachment. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included. The virtualization layer is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V. The lack of bundled local storage and GPU hardware provides a standard resource density profile, offering qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that rely entirely on external block storage.

In benchmark testing, the single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is in the top tier (top 10%), while multi-core CPU performance falls in the average tier. Geekbench single-core and text processing scores are strong (top 25%), whereas multi-core, software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing benchmarks are average. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with PassMark memory and database operations, consistently rank in the average tier.

For language model tasks, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds with small 135M models, but prompt processing for medium 7B models is weak (bottom 25%). Given the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average multi-core performance, this server is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and moderate database operations, though it lacks local storage and GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D8as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and lacks local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks and strong Geekbench single-core results. However, its multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier. It also exhibits weak prompt processing performance for medium-sized language models. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and moderate database tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8as_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suited for web serving and moderate database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8as_v6 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose VM with 32 GB RAM, AMD EPYC processors, and top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: An 8-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine with dedicated AMD EPYC processors, balanced memory, and top-tier single-core performance.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per vCPU)
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
Suited for general-purpose computing, static web serving, and moderate database tasks
